title: Alert in the Event of Audit Logging Process Failure
description: Alert in the event of an audit logging process failure.

checks.name: Audit Failure Alerts are Configured
checks.purpose: Verify the system is configured to generate an alert or take action when the Security event log is full or audit failures occur (CrashOnAuditFail setting)

checks.name: Event Log Full Action is Configured
checks.purpose: Confirm the Security event log retention method does not silently overwrite events without alerting; validate max size and overflow action

checks.name: Audit Failure Alerts are Configured
checks.purpose: Verify CrashOnAuditFail or equivalent alerting mechanism is configured so audit failures are not silently ignored

checks.name: Event Log Full Action is Configured
checks.purpose: Confirm the Security event log is set to an appropriate size and the overflow action triggers an alert or archives logs before overwriting

checks.name: SIEM or Monitoring Agent is Alerting on Audit Service Failures
checks.purpose: Verify a SIEM or monitoring tool is configured to alert on Windows Event ID 1102 (audit log cleared) and 1100 (audit service stopped)

checks.name: Auditd Disk Full Action is Configured
checks.purpose: Verify disk_full_action in /etc/audit/auditd.conf is set to halt, syslog, or email to prevent silent audit failures when disk is full

checks.name: Auditd Space Left Action is Configured
checks.purpose: Confirm space_left_action and admin_space_left_action in auditd.conf are set to email or halt to provide early warning before disk exhaustion

checks.name: Auditd Failure Flag is Set
checks.purpose: Verify auditd rules include -f 2 (panic on failure) or -f 1 (syslog on failure) to define behavior when audit subsystem encounters an error

checks.name: Auditd Disk Full Action is Configured
checks.purpose: Verify disk_full_action in /etc/audit/auditd.conf is set to halt, syslog, or email to prevent silent audit failures when disk is full

checks.name: Auditd Space Left Action is Configured
checks.purpose: Confirm space_left_action and admin_space_left_action in auditd.conf are set to email or halt to provide early warning before disk exhaustion

checks.name: Auditd Failure Flag is Set
checks.purpose: Verify auditd rules include -f 2 (panic on failure) or -f 1 (syslog on failure) to define behavior when audit subsystem encounters an error

remediation: Configure CrashOnAuditFail on Windows to halt or alert on audit failure. Set event log max size appropriately. On Linux, configure disk_full_action, space_left_action, and admin_space_left_action in auditd.conf. Integrate audit service failure events into SIEM alerting.
